Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Book: The Emerging Science of Machine Learning Benchmarks 122 upvotes, 6 comments
  2. OpenRocket 575 upvotes, 101 comments
  3. Show HN: I built 48 lightweight SVG backgrounds you can copy/paste 281 upvotes, 55 comments
  4. Nvidia greenboost: transparently extend GPU VRAM using system RAM/NVMe 346 upvotes, 71 comments
  5. Autoresearch for SAT Solvers 131 upvotes, 25 comments
  6. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 289 upvotes, 73 comments
  7. Conway's Game of Life, in real life 150 upvotes, 35 comments
  8. Cook: A simple CLI for orchestrating Claude Code 209 upvotes, 56 comments
  9. Show HN: Duplicate 3 layers in a 24B LLM, logical deduction .22→.76. No training 127 upvotes, 40 comments
  10. Why Cloudflare rule order matters? 30 upvotes, 6 comments
  11. Czech Man's Stone in Barn's Foundations Is Rare Bronze Age Spearhead Mold 50 upvotes, 14 comments
  12. Rob Pike’s Rules of Programming (1989) 936 upvotes, 435 comments
  13. The Serial Safety Net: Efficient Concurrency Control on Modern Hardware 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. RX – a new random-access JSON alternative 96 upvotes, 39 comments
  15. LotusNotes 95 upvotes, 39 comments
  16. Stdwin: Standard window interface by Guido Van Rossum [pdf] 26 upvotes, 7 comments
  17. A sufficiently detailed spec is code 381 upvotes, 202 comments
  18. Warranty Void If Regenerated 356 upvotes, 204 comments
  19. The math that explains why bell curves are everywhere 139 upvotes, 74 comments
  20. Nvidia NemoClaw 321 upvotes, 217 comments
  21. Show HN: Pano, a bookmarking tool built around shareable shelves 20 upvotes, 7 comments
  22. 2% of ICML papers desk rejected because the authors used LLM in their reviews 48 upvotes, 24 comments
  23. CVE-2026-3888: Important Snap Flaw Enables Local Privilege Escalation to Root 139 upvotes, 89 comments
  24. Show HN: Browser grand strategy game for hundreds of players on huge maps 36 upvotes, 18 comments
  25. Mozilla to launch free built-in VPN in upcoming Firefox 149 138 upvotes, 98 comments
  26. What 81,000 people want from AI 137 upvotes, 110 comments
  27. Eniac, the First General-Purpose Digital Computer, Turns 80 20 upvotes, 10 comments
  28. OpenAI Has New Focus (on the IPO) 231 upvotes, 210 comments
  29. Austin’s surge of new housing construction drove down rents 562 upvotes, 659 comments
  30. Show HN: Will my flight have Starlink? 226 upvotes, 297 comments

Last generated 7:00pm, 19 March 2026. Source here.
